top_signals.title: ‘Watershed moment’: EV sales soar as oil price volatility drives away petrol car demand
top_signals.summary: Brits are racing to get their hands on electric vehicles after a surge in oil prices triggered by the war in Iran has driven away demand for petrol and diesel cars, one of the UK’s biggest used car supermarkets has said. London-listed Motorpoint, which has seen its shares jump on the back of bumper profits, [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The Bank, FCA and HM Treasury joint statement on Frontier AI models and cyber resilience
top_signals.summary: Statement from the Bank of England, Financial Conduct Authority and HM Treasury
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Wedding Inflation Has Brides Paying Witches For Perfect Days
top_signals.summary: Picture this: It’s the week of your wedding. You’re staring down a forecast of clouds and rain. What do you do? A lot of brides have taken to hiring a weather witch. Josyana Joshua explains. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: FCA and Bank of England set out shared vision for tokenisation in UK wholesale markets
top_signals.summary: The Financial Conduct Authority and the Bank of England set out a shared vision and seek industry views on the future of UK wholesale markets
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: More than 80 retail bosses urge Starmer to tackle youth unemployment crisis
top_signals.summary: More than 80 retail bosses have called on the Prime Minister to set out urgent measures to tackle youth unemployment, warning that the “ladder of opportunity for young people is wobbling”.  The chiefs of leading retailers including Tesco, Sainsbury’s and John Lewis have called on Sir Keir Starmer to safeguard the position of retail as [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: PRA announces ring-fence change to reduce costs
top_signals.summary: The Prudential Regulation Authority has today announced plans to consult on reforming rules around shared operational services for ring-fenced banks.

top_signals.title: Slides from Huw Pill’s fireside chat at NatWest
Operational resilience in a rapidly changing world − speech by Liz Oakes
top_signals.summary: Given at the KPMG and Fitch Ratings London Banking Summit

top_signals.title: The Importance of Choice in Payments − speech by Victoria Cleland
top_signals.summary: Given at Banknote Conference, Washington DC

top_signals.title: Old exposures, new actors: implications for monetary policy of the UK’s external imbalances − speech by Catherine L. Mann
top_signals.summary: Given at the London School of Economics and Political Science

top_signals.title: Guidance: Small business regulatory taskforce
top_signals.summary: The small business taskforce is a joint government‑industry group to develop practical recommendations to reduce regulatory burdens on small and medium‑sized enterprises (SMEs) and micro-businesses.

top_signals.title: Revolut faced orders to fix ‘deficiencies’ in product launches in Europe
top_signals.summary: Revolut faced a regulatory slap-down from the European Central Bank last year as restrictions were placed on its operations in the continent. Europe’s most valuable fintech company had its permission to release new products across the 27 countries covered in the European Economic Area temporarily suspended last summer. The bank was instructed to rectify “deficiencies” [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.
